items.area: Buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity
items.quote: Only 2 contact roles logged (Adriel Picard, Jeffrey Buzila)

metrics.narrative: SaskTel's stated objectives are rapid mass adoption, accelerated sourcing/contracting ROI, and 'quick identification of savings opportunities allowing higher savings in a shorter period' (SF Business_Objectives__c). The pain is framed in cost terms: 'Ariba is priced as a premium tool but does not perform accordingly,' with fully manual invoicing and 'most procurement processes require manual intervention' (SF Customer_Business_Problem__c). No dollar figure, headcount, or cycle-time number has ever been quantified by the buyer on a call, and this qualitative case has not been re-confirmed in any exchange in the last 90 days. Quantifying the savings case with SaskTel's own numbers is an open action.

champion.narrative: No champion has emerged in 18 months. Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker role) has been largely unresponsive to Zycus outreach since mid-2025, and Jeffrey Buzila, who has driven most recent RFP-status communication, has shown process updates only ('the team is still reviewing responses to tally up scores,' 4 Jun 2026) with no advocacy, internal introductions, or agenda-driving evidenced. Two Horizon-event invites (5 May and 4 Jun 2026) and a third resent today (8 Jul 2026) are unanswered, indicating passive rather than active engagement from both named contacts. Building a genuine champion is the single highest-leverage MEDDPICC gap on this deal.

competition.narrative: SaskTel is running a formal four-vendor RFP naming Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba (SF Competitors__c), with SAP Ariba as the incumbent being displaced ('Procurement Tool: Ariba (under evaluation for replacement)', SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c). Zycus's positioned wedge is AI-powered automation, a unified end-to-end suite versus Ariba's fragmented setup, and a more usable supplier portal (SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), but no call or email has surfaced a buyer-stated preference among the four. An early qualification-era risk note also flagged that a fifth vendor could be considered (SF Why_not_Zycus__c) though it never appeared in the official four-vendor field. No competitive intelligence has been gathered since the 11 Mar 2026 RFP submission, so the wedge has not been tested against real buyer feedback.

identify_pain.narrative: SaskTel's documented pain is specific and vendor-anchored: 'Ariba is priced as a premium tool but does not perform accordingly,' invoicing is fully manual, and vendors dislike Ariba's portal, causing engagement difficulty (SF Customer_Business_Problem__c). Adriel Picard's team is named as the pain owner, seeking to replace Ariba with a unified S2P suite, first captured on the 28 Jan 2025 introductory call context ('looking to replace Ariba and need full S2P suite; all other processes are manual', SF Next_Step_History__c). This pain has not been independently re-confirmed by the buyer on any call in the last 90 days, so it should be re-validated as still current before betting the win on it.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced anywhere: Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, and there is no mention of MSA, legal review, or procurement-portal requirements beyond the Ariba RFP portal used for this evaluation. With no demo, presentation, or EB conversation completed in 18 months, paper process is far off; SOW/MSA and InfoSec have not been scoped or discussed. Nothing currently gives visibility into SaskTel's contracting requirements.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or C-level contact has ever engaged: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is unchecked and Who_will_approve_budget__c is blank (SF fields). SaskTel's account carries two CFO-level contacts, but neither has been linked to or appeared on this opportunity. Adriel Picard, Director of Procurement, holds the Decision Maker contact role but is a process owner, not the budget authority for a $357,898 multi-year S2P purchase; the deal-creation note that 'budget is already approved' (SF Compelling_Event__c) has never been re-validated. The 8 Jul 2026 Next Step now proposes reaching the CTO or the EVP directly to determine steering-committee membership, which is the right move to close this gap.

decision_process.narrative: The process is fully dated and buyer-run: RFP issued 11 Feb 2026, questions closed 25 Feb, submission 11 Mar 2026, shortlist announced week of 6 Apr 2026, and supplier presentations planned week of 20 Apr 2026 (SF Next_Step_History__c). The presentation round never occurred and is now roughly 11 weeks overdue as of 8 Jul 2026, with Jeffrey Buzila indicating on 4 Jun 2026 the team was still tallying scores. No approval-chain fields are populated, so the internal sign-off path after presentations is unmapped. The single largest process risk is that this stalled gate never gets rescheduled before the 4 Sep 2026 close date.

decision_criteria.narrative: SaskTel ran this as a formal, dated RFP (issued 11 Feb 2026, questions due 18 Feb, submission 11 Mar 2026 at 2:00pm CST) rather than a workshop-based fit exercise, so criteria live in the RFP scorecard rather than a BRD (SF Next_Step_History__c). Business_Requirements__c and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are both blank/false, meaning Zycus has no visibility into the actual weighted criteria SaskTel's evaluators are scoring against. The clearest documented criterion is that SaskTel flagged AI as 'Very Critical' with a Tier 2 'Info Assist (AI Insights)' rating in the RFP itself (SF AIS_Why__c). Requesting the scorecard or a debrief once shortlisting resolves would close this gap.

day_summary.overall: The only signal today is a big close-date slip on SaskTel, pushed from end of April all the way to September — over four months out. No emails or calls logged alongside it, so this reads as a quiet re-forecasting rather than a specific event; the deal has effectively been parked, and someone should find out what's actually stalled before treating the new date as real.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: SaskTel is a $357,898 multi-year S2P opportunity sitting in Shortlisted against a four-vendor RFP field (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER), with a 4 Sep 2026 close date now 57 days out. The relationship is single-threaded through a Director of Procurement and an RFP coordinator, with no economic buyer, champion, or pain case established, and the supplier-presentation round that should have run the week of 20 Apr 2026 is still unscheduled roughly 11 weeks later.

deal_risk.text: 57 days remain to the 4 Sep 2026 close date, but the presentation round, commercial discussion, EB approval, and contracting have not started, and the presentation step alone is already ~11 weeks overdue; the recorded Best Case forecast is not supported by the remaining path.
No economic buyer is mapped or engaged for a $357,898 multi-year commitment.
Single-threaded relationship (Adriel Picard, Jeffrey Buzila) with only 2 contact roles logged on the opportunity.

win_position.text: No economic buyer or C-level SaskTel contact is engaged; Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker role) is the senior-most confirmed contact, and the deal owner's 8 Jul 2026 Next Step is still trying to determine whether the CTO or CIO/EVP sit on the steering committee.
The supplier-presentation round scheduled for the week of 20 Apr 2026 has not occurred as of 9 Jul 2026 (~11 weeks overdue), with no confirmed new date.
No champion, pain, or quantified business case is on record; the only Avoma-read call (28 Jan 2025) was a purely social introduction with no discovery content.
Zycus cleared the formal RFP gate and was named to the shortlist (week of 6 Apr 2026), confirming it remains an active, credible vendor in a four-way field.
The RFP field includes SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide and JAGGAER with zero competitive differentiation intelligence gathered to date.

deal_momentum.text: The only confirmed two-way buyer touch in recent months is the buyer-side email reply on 13 Apr 2026 (87 days ago); two subsequent Horizon event invites (4 May, 4 Jun 2026) went unanswered.
The buyer-side coordinator's stated late-Apr/early-May 2026 window for next steps (14 Apr 2026 note) has passed with no follow-through communicated.
The deal owner resent the Horizon invite to Adriel Picard on 8 Jul 2026 (yesterday) and set a dated trigger to escalate to the CTO/CIO if there is no response, keeping the account actively worked.
No forward-dated meeting, demo, or milestone is currently confirmed with SaskTel.

customer_commitment.text: Beyond submitting the RFP response (11 Mar 2026) and confirming shortlisting, SaskTel has made no forward commitment: the presentation round is unscheduled and neither Horizon invite has been accepted.
The buyer-side coordinator has continued to respond periodically (14 Apr, 4 Jun 2026 notes) confirming the evaluation is still active internally, even though it has slowed.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: SaskTel's stated objectives align closely with Zycus's core pitch (unified AI-driven S2P suite), but none of it has been demo-validated in 18 months, so alignment remains paper-only.

hard.ais_why:
• This is a text-based RFP with no recorded calls, so all evidence comes from the Salesforce record. The strongest written signal is that Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for SaskTel, paired with a Tier 2 "Info Assist (AI Insights)" rating for what they want from AI in the RFP.
• Read together, those two fields say SaskTel's procurement team self-rated AI as a Very Critical evaluation criterion and explicitly wants AI Insights baked into the S2P scope. That self-authored RFP positioning is meaningful buyer-side AI pull, even without a verbatim quote, and it's enough to lift this off the resistant floor.
• What caps the score: the AI tier is Insights, not Agentic or Autonomous; no demo, POC, or champion has been confirmed because there are no Avoma calls; and a text RFP can mask whether the Very Critical rating is genuine excitement or procurement-checklist hygiene. Confidence stays Low until live engagement validates.

ai_critical_signals.text: SaskTel is running a formal four-vendor RFP naming Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba as of the 11 Mar 2026 submission, with no buyer preference stated and scores still being tallied as of 4 Jun 2026.

The only recorded call was a purely social introductory chat on 28 Jan 2025; the real substance has all run through email, most recently Jeffrey Buzila's 13 Apr 2026 note that the team was closing year-end books before deciding next steps.
The deal owner is now trying to confirm through the invite process whether the CTO and the CIO/EVP sit on SaskTel's S2P steering committee, but neither has ever engaged with Zycus directly.
No pricing, ROI, or budget-authority conversation has taken place with SaskTel in any call, email, or field despite an early note that budget was approved back in early 2025.

customer_preference.evidence: No competitive intel or buyer-stated preference has surfaced in any call, email, or field; RFP scoring has been silent since 4 Jun 2026 (SF Next_Step_History__c).

champion_strength.evidence: No advocacy or agenda-driving seen from either named contact since 13 Apr 2026 (SF Next_Step_History__c).

economic_buyer_engagement.evidence: No CFO/C-level contact linked to this opp despite two CFOs identified at the account level.

ai_deal_scores_evidence.summary: SaskTel's S2P RFP has been shortlisted for over 7 months with zero economic-buyer engagement and 86 days of buyer silence; the single biggest risk is that the promised presentation round never gets scheduled before the 4 Sep 2026 close date.

deal_risk.text: With 58 days to the 4 Sep 2026 close date and the presentation round already ~11 weeks overdue, there is no realistic remaining runway for commercial negotiation, EB sign-off, and contracting.
No economic buyer, CFO, or C-level SaskTel contact (despite two CFOs identified at the account level) has ever been linked to or engaged on this opportunity.

win_position.text: SaskTel shortlisted Zycus in the four-vendor S2P RFP back on 24 Nov 2025, but the supplier-presentation round planned for the week of 20 Apr 2026 never happened and no economic buyer or CFO has ever engaged on this opportunity.

Jeffrey Buzila told the deal owner on 4 Jun 2026 the team was still tallying RFP scores, but neither he nor Adriel Picard has replied to three consecutive Horizon-event invites (5 May, 4 Jun, 8 Jul 2026).

Zycus remains in the named four-vendor shortlist (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) with no evidence any rival has been preferred or selected as of 8 Jul 2026, so the door to win is still open.

deal_momentum.text: The deal owner re-sent the Horizon invite to Adriel Picard on 8 Jul 2026 after getting no reply to the 5 May and 4 Jun 2026 invites, and plans to contact the CTO and CIO/EVP directly if there is still no response.

The last genuine two-way buyer touch was Jeffrey Buzila's 13 Apr 2026 email saying next steps would follow once SaskTel closed its year-end books; that window passed roughly ten weeks ago with no follow-through.

customer_commitment.text: Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker role) and Jeffrey Buzila are the only two contact roles logged on this opportunity, and neither has driven the process forward since 13 Apr 2026.
Jeffrey Buzila is the only SaskTel contact who has proactively updated Zycus on RFP-scoring status, most recently on 4 Jun 2026.
